_comment: TYPE-THE-WORD EXERCISE (type: type) — learner types the full Kanien'kéha word from an English prompt. The most demanding production exercise. 114 in the corpus.
_wid: Glossary ID of the word being tested. Required.
prompt: How do you say 'leaf' in Kanien'kéha?
_prompt: Give the English meaning and ask for the Kanien'kéha word. Vary phrasing across lessons. Alternatives: 'Type the Kanien'kéha word for [english]', 'What is the Kanien'kéha word for [english]?'
_sub: Use 'Dialect variants accepted' whenever alts includes community variants. Use 'All accents accepted' if only unaccented variants are in alts. Optional but strongly recommended — it reassures learners their form is valid.
_correct: The canonical form. Case-insensitive matching is applied by the app. Accent marks are checked — include them in correct.
_alts: All other accepted strings. Always include the unaccented lowercase form. Add dialect variants (r→l, y→i, etc.) where they apply. The app accepts any string in alts as correct. Required — minimum one entry.
cm: Niawenhkó:wa! Ohón:ta' — leaf. The length marker (:) on the ó changes the meaning if dropped — always include it in writing.
_cm: Correct message. Confirm the word and add one brief note about spelling, pronunciation, or morphology. Required.
wm: That answer is incorrect — please try again. The word is Ohón:ta' — note the length marker on the ó.
_wm: Wrong-answer message. Always include the correct answer spelled out. Required.

_optional_image._comment: image is valid on type exercises but rare (23 exercises total use images across mc, fill, and type combined). Omit unless a visual genuinely aids recall.
